summary: Applied the unified platform API gateway live by reconciling the docker-runtime gateway stack, fixing the edge-publication idempotence bug that blocked `api.example.com`, expanding the shared edge certificate, and verifying both anonymous and authenticated public API paths.

verification.observed: `ansible-playbook -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump playbooks/api-gateway.yml` completed successfully and verified an authenticated `/v1/platform/services` call during role verification.
The live `lv3-edge` certificate SAN list now includes `DNS:api.example.com` after the targeted edge-publication rerun.
A valid `lv3-agent-hub` bearer token issued from the live Keycloak realm was accepted by `https://api.example.com/v1/platform/services`, which returned `HTTP 200` with `24` services.

notes: The `nginx_edge_publication` role had an idempotence bug: `/etc/nginx/sites-enabled/lv3-edge.conf` already existed as a plain file, so the role failed before certificate expansion. The repo now forces the managed symlink replacement and the targeted rerun succeeded.

The gateway runtime now uses the shared `keycloak_default` Docker network for JWKS access, verifies a real bearer token during converge, and self-heals a missing published host port by forcing a compose recreate.

Controller-side client-credentials requests to `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3/protocol/openid-connect/token` for `lv3-agent-hub` still returned `unauthorized_client` during this rollout, but the live realm itself issued a valid token on the runtime host and that token was accepted by the public gateway surface.
